food_subgroup: White wheat flour bread — plain baguette, 1 slice ~50g

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for plain French baguette; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.description: 1 regular slice (~50g), approximately 4 diagonal slices from a 250g standard baguette
serving.natural_language_portion: One regular diagonal slice of French baguette weighing approximately 50g, as typically served in a restaurant bread basket or French bistro in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: French Baguette — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 172683)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172683 is a direct match for plain French baguette (white wheat flour). All per-100g nutrient values are sourced from this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — French baguette (white wheat flour, plain)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 75 for plain French baguette sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. This is the highest GI of any commonly available bread type. GL per 50g serving = (75 × 26.9g available carbohydrate) / 100 = ~19.5. Available carbohydrate per 50g serving = 53.8 × 0.5 = 26.9g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — 550mg per 100g (~275mg per 50g slice, ~14% of WHO 2,000mg daily limit per slice)
hypertension.guidance: Limit to 1–2 slices per meal; a restaurant bread basket of 3 slices contributes 825mg sodium before the main course, which is over 40% of the WHO daily sodium limit and should be avoided by hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI 75 (highest of all common bread types) and GL ~19.5 per 50g slice — produces the sharpest postprandial glucose spike of any common bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: T2DM patients should avoid French baguette as a regular bread choice; if unavoidable at a restaurant, limit to 1 slice (~50g) eaten mid-meal rather than before the meal, and request sourdough or rye bread as a lower-GI alternative where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate content (53.8g/100g), high GI (75), and elevated sodium (550mg/100g) with negligible saturated fat (0.2g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high GI and refined carbohydrate load contribute to postprandial triglyceride elevation and endothelial stress; CVD patients should limit portion size to 1 slice and prefer whole-grain or sourdough alternatives to reduce glycaemic and sodium burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: French baguette (plain) is a traditional French white wheat flour bread characterised by a long, thin cylindrical shape (~65cm × 6cm), a thin crackling golden crust (croûte), and an open, irregular soft crumb (mie). It is made from only four ingredients: white wheat flour, water, salt, and yeast — containing near-zero fat and minimal dietary fibre. Per 1 regular slice (~50g): approximately 135 kcal, 4.5g protein, 0.45g fat, 26.9g carbohydrate, 1.0g dietary fibre, 275mg sodium. The baguette has the HIGHEST glycemic index (GI ~75) of any commonly available bread type — higher than white sandwich bread (~71) and comparable to white rice (~72). Glycemic load per 50g slice is ~19.5; a restaurant bread basket of 3 slices = GL ~58.5, which exceeds half the total daily GL target for T2DM management before the main course is served. In Singapore, baguettes are served at French bistros (Brasserie Gavroche, db Bistro, Saveur), in European restaurant bread baskets, and are sold at Tiong Bahru Bakery, Antoinette, and major supermarkets. The modern elongated baguette form was standardised in Paris in the 1920s. For T2DM and CVD patients, sourdough or rye bread is strongly preferred. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172683; GI from University of Sydney GI Database.
common_user_queries: French bread vs white bread GI

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172683, an exact match for plain French baguette (white wheat flour). GI value of 75 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database, the authoritative GI reference. All core macros and secondary nutrients are fully populated from the approved primary source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for French baguette not separately reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172683 at the level of MUFA/PUFA split. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source entry used. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy. cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 — plain baguette contains no animal-derived fat; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed 0.0 for plain white wheat flour baguette per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172683. GL per serving calculated as: GI (75) × available carbohydrate per 50g serving (26.9g) / 100 = 19.5. This food is a Western / French cuisine item; Singaporean multilingual naming requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply. No mandatory local-language names are required for French cuisine under Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules.
